clinical_coherence.notes: 74M African American with HTN, moderate aortic stenosis, DM2, CKD 3b, and BPH presenting after exertional syncope while climbing stairs. HR 32 on presentation. Calcific aortic stenosis with conduction system extension as mechanism. Prior syncopal episode at church 2 weeks earlier adds recurrence pattern. Exertional trigger consistent with hemodynamic compromise from combined AS and CHB.

tool_output_consistency.notes: ECG shows complete heart block with ventricular escape at 32 bpm, wide QRS with RBBB morphology, and prolonged QTc 512 ms. EEG normal. Labs show elevated troponin (demand), elevated BNP (480), elevated creatinine above baseline. Holter confirms persistent CHB. All outputs concordant with calcific conduction disease.

ground_truth_quality.notes: R55 is acceptable but I44.2 would be more specific. Primary diagnosis correctly identifies calcific AS with conduction system involvement. Differential includes AS-related syncope as a contributing factor, which is nuanced and correct.

difficulty_calibration.notes: Straightforward is appropriate. Complete heart block on ECG with HR 32 is immediately diagnostic. The aortic stenosis adds a contributing mechanism but the CHB is the primary finding.

demographic_plausibility.notes: 74M with extensive cardiovascular comorbidities is realistic. Moderate AS progressing to conduction disease is a well-recognized pathophysiological pathway. Medications appropriate for his conditions.

educational_value.notes: Good teaching case demonstrating calcific conduction disease from aortic stenosis. However, very similar to SYNC-CARD-S01 in clinical pattern (elderly male with CHB), limiting incremental value.

issues_found: Minor: R55 ICD code is symptom-level rather than etiology-specific (I44.2)
Clinical pattern closely resembles SYNC-CARD-S01, limiting case set diversity
strengths: Aortic stenosis as mechanism for conduction disease is pathophysiologically sound
Ethnic diversity with African American patient
